data.title: Salesforce’s Anthropic Bet And What It Could Mean For CRM Valuation
data.description:
• Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) was an early investor in Anthropic, the AI company behind the Claude large language models.
  • Anthropic is reportedly preparing for a public listing that could carry a very large valuation.
  • This ties Salesforce directly to one of the most talked about AI IPO candidates in the current market.

Salesforce runs a large customer relationship management platform and has been incorporating AI into sales, service, marketing and analytics tools for years. Its early backing of Anthropic highlights how CRM is using its venture arm to connect with core AI infrastructure, not just application-level features.

For investors tracking NYSE:CRM, Anthropic’s potential IPO raises questions about possible financial outcomes from the stake and about how closely Salesforce can integrate Claude models into its products over time. It also focuses attention on how CRM might use any future capital returned from this investment, whether for additional AI partnerships, product development or other priorities.

data.title: IBM Expands MIT AI And Quantum Research Link To Enterprise Delivery
data.description:
• IBM (NYSE:IBM) and MIT launched the MIT IBM Computing Research Lab, expanding their collaboration in AI, algorithms, and quantum computing.
  • The new lab focuses on foundational research that seeks to move beyond current classical computing limits, with potential applications across sectors such as healthcare and materials science.
  • IBM plans to create 750 tech roles and open its FutureNow innovation center within the Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park in Chicago.

For investors tracking large enterprise technology platforms, this move reinforces IBM's position in AI, quantum computing, and cybersecurity as core lines of business. The expanded MIT partnership puts more emphasis on early stage research, while the Chicago tech center ties that work to practical use cases and customer demand. Together, these efforts sit alongside IBM's existing software, consulting, and infrastructure offerings.

Looking ahead, readers may want to watch how research output from the MIT IBM lab flows into products, services, and partnerships, and how quickly the Chicago center ramps hiring and customer activity. The link between academic research, a growing talent pool, and on-the-ground enterprise projects could influence IBM's role in areas such as AI workloads, quantum experimentation, and security-focused solutions over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for International Business Machines by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on International Business Machines.

We've flagged 1 risk for International Business Machines. See which could impact your investment.

This MIT IBM expansion and the planned FutureNow Chicago center point in the same direction for International Business Machines, tying long-horizon AI and quantum research to client-focused delivery. The Cambridge lab extends IBM’s existing work with MIT into algorithms and quantum, which sits close to IBM’s broader push in quantum centric supercomputing and AI heavy software like IBM Bob. On the other side, the Chicago hub and 750 planned roles in AI, cybersecurity, data science, and quantum put more execution capacity closer to enterprise customers, similar to how IBM is building out consulting and security offerings with partners such as Entrust. For you as an investor, the key question is whether this academic pipeline and regional hiring translate into differentiated software, recurring services, and higher value consulting work, especially when large peers such as Microsoft, Amazon, and Google are also investing heavily in AI research and cloud based delivery. The timing also intersects with Q1 2026 results and a long history of research led projects at MIT, which already produced more than 1,500 peer reviewed articles and hundreds of funded students and researchers under the prior Watson AI Lab.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The International Business Machines Narrative

  • The expanded MIT IBM Computing Research Lab supports the narrative that IBM’s focus on hybrid cloud, AI, and advanced hardware like the z17 can feed future high margin software and infrastructure offerings tied to complex workloads in areas such as materials science and biology.
  • The commitment to 750 Chicago roles and a physical innovation center raises the execution bar in Consulting and Software, areas the narrative already flags as exposed to macro swings and competition from hyperscale cloud providers.
  • The MIT research output and Chicago talent pipeline, especially in quantum algorithms and neuromorphic style computing concepts, may not yet be fully reflected in narrative assumptions that focus mainly on current hybrid cloud and generative AI product sets.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for International Business Machines to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ IBM has been flagged as carrying a high level of debt, so funding long-duration research collaborations and a new Chicago center could add pressure if cash flows from software and consulting do not keep pace.
  • ⚠️ The AI and quantum research focus directly overlaps with efforts from Microsoft, Amazon, and Google, so IBM faces the risk that academic breakthroughs or enterprise wins accrue to competitors instead of translating into differentiated IBM platforms.
  • 🎁 The MIT IBM Computing Research Lab extends a track record of over 210 projects and 1,500 peer reviewed articles, which gives IBM early access to foundational AI and quantum work that could inform future products and services.
  • 🎁 The FutureNow Chicago center and apprenticeship program with City Colleges of Chicago expand IBM’s access to trained talent in AI, quantum, and cybersecurity, which can support execution of complex client projects and strengthen long-term customer relationships.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on whether IBM can point to concrete examples where MIT IBM research feeds into commercial offerings, such as quantum enabled simulations, more efficient AI models, or sector specific tools in healthcare and materials science. It is also worth watching hiring progress and client wins at the FutureNow Chicago center, and whether management begins to attribute software, consulting, or security revenue to this hub on future calls. Comparing IBM’s AI and quantum proof points and commercial references with those from Microsoft, Amazon, and Google can help you gauge how this collaboration and expansion are shaping IBM’s competitive position.

data.title: Alamos Gold (TSX:AGI) Net Margin Surges To 51.2% Reinforcing Bullish Profitability Narrative
data.description:
Alamos Gold (TSX:AGI) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$596.7 million and basic EPS of US$0.46, set against trailing 12 month revenue of US$2.1 billion and EPS of US$2.53 that were attributed to a 312.6% year over year jump in earnings and a 5 year annualized earnings growth rate of 59.7% per year. Over the last reported quarters, revenue moved from US$333 million in Q1 2025 to US$575.3 million in Q4 2025 and then to US$596.7 million in Q1 2026, while quarterly EPS shifted from US$0.04 to US$1.03 and then US$0.46, with trailing profitability supported by a net margin of 51.2% versus 18.4% a year earlier. Overall, the latest results come after a period where earnings, margins, and revenue have been key factors in how investors are likely to interpret this update.

See our full analysis for Alamos Gold.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results align with the main stories investors follow around Alamos Gold and where those narratives may need to be reconsidered.

Margins Backed By 51.2% Net Profit

  • Over the last 12 months, Alamos Gold produced US$2.1b in revenue and US$1.1b in net income, which equates to a 51.2% net margin compared with 18.4% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative points to higher margin potential from integrating high grade Island Gold ore into the larger Magino mill. The current 51.2% trailing margin lines up with that view, although gold production over the last two reported quarters moved from 4.41 million ounces to 3.85 million ounces, which shows how closely those margins are tied to efficient operations and grade.

Revenue Near US$600m, Production Mixed

  • Q1 2026 revenue of US$596.7m came alongside gold production of 3.85 million ounces. Q3 and Q2 2025 showed 4.41 million and 4.27 million ounces respectively on lower quarterly revenue, so volume and price are clearly both at work in the top line.
  • Consensus narrative talks about production expansion at Magino and Island Gold pushing annual output towards 900,000 to 1,000,000 ounces and supporting higher cash flow. The recent production data, with 16.96 million ounces over the trailing year versus 17.64 million ounces a few quarters earlier, reminds you that even with strong earnings growth of over 300% in the last year, execution at each mine has to line up with those growth plans.
    • Analysts also expect revenue to grow 17.6% per year and earnings 16.8% per year, which contrasts with the quarter to quarter swings in production you can already see in the reported figures.
    • The bullish view that expansion will steadily lift volumes is therefore partly supported by the strong trailing earnings, but the production pattern shows there is room for variability across periods.
On the bullish side, investors who focus on that 312.6% earnings growth and strong margins might want to see how the full optimistic thesis ties these numbers to future projects and gold market views before deciding what it means for them personally. 🐂 Alamos Gold Bull Case

P/E Of 15.8x And Big Gap To DCF Fair Value

  • At a share price of CA$54.20, Alamos trades on a 15.8x P/E against a Canadian Metals & Mining average of 16.4x and a peer average of 19.7x. The stock also sits far below a DCF fair value of CA$209.79 and an analyst target of CA$82.38.
  • Bears highlight execution and cost risks at Island Gold and Magino, as well as sensitivity to gold prices. The valuation gap, with the market price well below both DCF fair value and the analyst target, shows that despite trailing net income of US$1.1b and a 59.7% 5 year annualized earnings growth rate, investors are still pricing in those risks and not simply extrapolating the recent 312.6% earnings expansion.
    • Analysts’ implied upside of roughly 52% to their target from CA$54.20 indicates that even after accounting for project and commodity risk, their expectations for earnings and revenue growth justify a higher valuation than the current P/E suggests.
    • For a reader, the key tension is that the strong trailing margin and earnings record supports the bullish case, while the discount to both peers and fair value estimates is consistent with cautious views around cost inflation and project delivery.

data.title: Aichi Electric (NSE:6623) Earnings Growth And 6.6% Margin Challenge Discounted Valuation Narrative
data.description:
Aichi Electric (NSE:6623) has wrapped up FY 2026 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥36.6 billion and basic EPS of ¥235.37, while trailing 12 month revenue sits at ¥129.4 billion with EPS of ¥907.43. Over the past year, the company has seen revenue move from ¥120.3 billion to ¥129.4 billion and trailing EPS advance from ¥710.99 to ¥907.43, alongside a net profit margin of 6.6% and described high earnings quality that support a more efficient earnings base. Taken together, these results indicate a period in which profitability and income generation have held up for shareholders.

27.6% earnings growth and margin at 6.6%

  • Over the last 12 months, Aichi Electric generated ¥8,527 million in net income and a 6.6% net margin, with trailing earnings up 27.6% compared with the prior year.
  • What supports a bullish angle is that this 27.6% trailing earnings growth sits alongside a 10.7% per year five year earnings growth rate and a 6.6% margin, which bulls can point to as a mix of scale and profitability, while critics can still question how repeatable that pace is given there is only one year of the higher growth figure in view.
    • Supporters often highlight that both the 27.6% trailing growth and the 10.7% multi year pace are tied to actual net income numbers, from ¥6,684 million a year earlier to ¥8,527 million now.

  • What challenges a simple bearish take on volatility is that, while skeptics might focus on EPS shifting between quarters, the step up from ¥710.99 to ¥907.43 on a trailing 12 month basis and net income rising from ¥6,684 million to ¥8,527 million shows those swings are occurring around a higher full year base, even though the exact drivers behind each quarter are not broken out here.

    • Supporters can counter that trailing EPS rising by almost ¥200 over the year suggests the business exited FY 2026 with more earnings per share than at the same point a year ago, despite quarter to quarter shifts.

data.title: Global Security Experts (TSE:4417) Margin Expansion Reinforces Bullish Profitability Narratives
data.description:

The company has seen revenue move from ¥8.8b and EPS of ¥67.23 on a trailing basis in FY 2025 to ¥11.0b and ¥98.80 respectively in FY 2026, while quarterly revenue stepped from ¥2.5b and EPS of ¥19.59 in FY 2025 Q4 to ¥3.2b and ¥32.42 in FY 2026 Q4. This frames a year in which higher margins did a lot of the heavy lifting for shareholders.

  • Over the trailing 12 months, Global Security Experts converted ¥11,022 million of revenue into ¥1,486 million of net income, which works out to a 13.5% net margin compared with 11.5% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that this margin profile sits alongside a 47.1% earnings gain over the past year, yet net income stepped up quarter by quarter in FY 2026 from ¥200 million in Q1 to ¥488 million in Q4. This suggests that earnings quality in the data is backed by consistent quarterly profitability rather than a single spike.
    • Supporters can also point to trailing EPS moving from ¥67.23 to ¥98.80 over roughly the same period, which aligns with that 47.1% earnings growth and the higher margin.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Veradermics (MANE) After A 162% Year To Date Surge?
data.description:
• Wondering if Veradermics at around US$99 a share still lines up with the story you have in mind, or if the price is already baking in a lot of optimism.
  • The stock has seen sharp recent moves, with returns of 43% over the last 7 days, 56.8% over the last 30 days, and 162.3% year to date. This can change how you think about both risk and potential reward.
  • Recent headlines around Veradermics have focused on its position in the pharmaceuticals space and investor interest in its prospects, helping to frame these strong short term returns. This context matters because sentiment shifts can influence pricing just as much as fundamentals.

Veradermics scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Veradermics Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to what they might be worth in today’s dollars. It is essentially asking what a stream of future cash flows is worth right now.

For Veradermics, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $71.63 million. Analyst and model projections then move through a period of negative free cash flows, such as $69 million in 2026 and $95.50 million in 2027, before reaching a projected free cash flow of $123 million in 2030. Beyond the analyst window, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows out to 2035 using its own growth assumptions.

Combining all of these discounted cash flows results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $250.72 per share. Against a current share price around $99, the DCF output implies the stock trades at roughly a 60.5% discount to this estimate, which indicates a wide gap between price and modeled value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Veradermics is undervalued by 60.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 51 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Veradermics.

Approach 2: Veradermics Price vs Book

For companies in pharmaceuticals, price to book, or P/B, can be a useful yardstick because it compares what you pay in the market with the accounting value of net assets on the balance sheet. For profitable, established businesses, investors often look for a P/B that reflects both the quality of those assets and the earnings they can support.

Growth expectations and risk also shape what feels like a normal P/B level. Faster growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty can point to a lower one. This is why simple comparisons can be tricky.

Veradermics currently trades on a P/B of 30.09x, which sits well above the Pharmaceuticals industry average of 2.63x and the peer group average of 4.33x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what P/B might make sense given Veradermics’ earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risks. Because it pulls these company specific factors together, the Fair Ratio can be a more tailored benchmark than broad industry or peer averages.

In this case, Veradermics’ actual P/B of 30.09x is meaningfully higher than the Fair Ratio. This points to the shares screening as expensive on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Meet Narratives, a simple way to put a clear story behind your numbers by linking your view of a company to its forecast revenue, earnings, margins and ultimately a fair value estimate.

A Narrative connects three pieces in one place: the company story you believe, the financial forecast that story implies and the fair value you arrive at. This allows you to quickly compare that value to the current share price and decide whether the gap looks appealing or not.

Narratives are available on Simply Wall St’s Community page, used by millions of investors, and they update automatically when new information such as news, filings or earnings is added. This helps ensure your view does not stay frozen while the facts move.

  • Consensus narrative expects efficiency gains from the Suncorp Bank acquisition and the shift to a lower cost dual platform to support margins, yet the recent rise in the cost to income ratio and the current margin level give a clear reference point for how much operating leverage those initiatives would need to offset.

  • Earnings are forecast to grow about 9.6% per year with revenue at about 4.5% per year, while ANZ trades on a trailing P/E of 18.2x versus domestic peers at 18.6x and the Global Banks industry at 10.9x, and a DCF fair value of A$40.80 compares with a current share price of A$35.61 and an analyst consensus price target of A$36.34.

    • Supporters of the bullish angle highlight that the DCF fair value of A$40.80 is around A$5 above the current A$35.61 share price and that earnings forecasts of about 9.6% per year sit alongside high assessed earnings quality, which some investors may see as support for the case that the current price leaves room if those forecasts are met.
